check.name: UncommentedMain
check.description:
Detects uncommented main methods.

Rationale: A main method is often used for debugging purposes. When debugging is finished, developers often forget to remove the method, which changes the API and increases the size of the resulting class or JAR file. Except for the real program entry points, all main methods should be removed or commented out of the sources.

Note: Compact source files (JEP 512) are skipped by design. The whole purpose of compact source files is to serve as standalone single-file programs with a main method as the entry point. Unlike regular classes where leftover main methods may be debugging artifacts, compact sources inherently require a main method to function.

property.name: excludedClasses
property.description: Specify pattern for qualified names of classes which are allowed to have a main method.
